title: Caramelised onion tart
subtitle: Shortcrust tart filled with slow-cooked onions, Gruyère, and thyme custard
excerpt: A savoury tart made with a shortcrust pastry case and a filling of deeply caramelised onions bound with a Gruyère and egg custard, seasoned with fresh thyme. The onions take 40 to 45 minutes to caramelise properly. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: Savoury egg-and-cream tarts in a shortcrust pastry case are documented in French regional cookery from the eighteenth century onward. Urbain Dubois, La Cuisine Classique (1856), public domain: an onion and egg custard tart (quiche à l'oignon) is documented as a standard savoury tart preparation. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: a baked custard tart made with caramelised onions in shortcrust pastry is documented as a supper preparation consistent with this recipe.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The caramelised onions can be made 2 days ahead and refrigerated. Assemble and bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; blind bake 15 minutes, then fill and bake 25–30 minutes until the custard is set with a slight tremble in the centre

content.text: Properly caramelised onions take 40 to 45 minutes over low heat; any shorter and they are merely softened, not caramelised. Resist the urge to raise the heat. The cooked onions should be deep brown, jammy, and sweet throughout. A splash of water added mid-cook helps lift the stuck bits from the pan and prevents burning.

Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} (filling portion) in a large frying pan over low heat. Add the {{onion}} and a pinch of the {{sea-salt-fine}} (custard portion). Stir to coat in butter, then leave, stirring every 5 to 8 minutes, for 40 to 45 minutes until deep brown and jammy. If the pan dries out, add a splash of water and stir to lift the caramelised bits from the bottom. Add the {{thyme-fresh}} leaves in the last 5 minutes. Season and leave to cool.
Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (pastry portion) into the {{plain-flour}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} (pastry portion) until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add the egg yolk and 2 tablespoons of cold water and press together until the dough coheres. Flatten, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 175°C fan. Roll to 3 mm thick, line the tart tin, and blind bake with paper and beans for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper.

Whisk the {{double-cream}}, {{eggs}} (custard portion), and {{sea-salt-fine}} (custard portion) together. Spread the cooled onions over the pastry base. Scatter the {{gruyere}} evenly over the onions. Pour the custard over; the filling should come to just below the rim. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until the custard is set with a gentle tremble in the centre. Rest for 15 minutes before serving.
